why_it_matters: Shows event loop, transform, browser automation, and worker CPU pressure
healthy_signal: Sustained average below 60 percent during normal business traffic
warning_signal: Sustained 70 percent or higher with rising latency
action: Add vCPU, split heavy workflows, or move sustained concurrency to queue workers

why_it_matters: Shows binary data, large JSON, Code node, AI payload, and browser process memory pressure
healthy_signal: High-water mark below 65 percent and returns to baseline after executions
warning_signal: Above 70 percent for long periods, rising trend, heap errors, or restarts
action: Change binary data mode, lower concurrency, add RAM, or isolate heavy workers

why_it_matters: Unexpected restarts reveal crash, memory, disk, or process failure
warning_signal: Any unplanned restart in production
action: Inspect logs, check OOM symptoms, reduce concurrency, and fix binary-heavy flows

why_it_matters: Execution data and database binary mode can grow the state layer
healthy_signal: Growth matches expected execution count and retention
warning_signal: Week-over-week growth exceeds forecast or backup window expands
action: Tune execution saving and pruning, move binary data out of DB, or increase DB storage

why_it_matters: Workers and webhook bursts can exhaust DB connections
healthy_signal: Connection use stays well below configured DB limit
warning_signal: Connections approach limit or queries wait for connections
action: Reduce workers, tune pool, increase DB capacity, or add queue controls

why_it_matters: Database CPU reveals query, pruning, execution history, and backup pressure
warning_signal: Sustained 70 percent or higher during ordinary workflow execution
action: Review execution data retention, slow workflows, backup timing, and DB tier

why_it_matters: Frequency determines DB writes, logs, storage, and API usage
healthy_signal: Count matches planned schedule and webhook volume
warning_signal: Unexpected spike or steady growth without capacity review
action: Review triggers, rate limit external inputs, and update sizing forecast

why_it_matters: Workflow duration shows whether external calls or internal processing are slowing
warning_signal: p95 rises after new binary, AI, or browser steps
action: Profile slow workflows, add timeouts, split background work, or use workers

why_it_matters: Failures often reveal API limits, DB errors, memory pressure, or storage limits
healthy_signal: Stable near zero for business-critical workflows
warning_signal: Error rate rises by workflow or node type
action: Inspect logs, identify failing node class, and fix capacity or retry settings

why_it_matters: Shows current concurrency pressure
warning_signal: Active count rises with latency or memory pressure
action: Set N8N_CONCURRENCY_PRODUCTION_LIMIT or move to queue mode

why_it_matters: Wait states preserve execution data and are not pruned while waiting
healthy_signal: Waiting count and age stay within policy
warning_signal: Waiting executions accumulate or old waiting executions carry large payloads
action: Review wait workflows, pass file references instead of files, and set age review

why_it_matters: Queue mode backlog shows workers are not keeping up
warning_signal: Depth grows continuously during ordinary load
action: Add workers, separate heavy worker pools, or lower incoming concurrency

why_it_matters: Worker memory isolates heavy workflow pressure
healthy_signal: Worker memory returns to baseline after jobs
warning_signal: Memory stays high or spikes during specific worker classes
action: Separate AI/browser/binary workers and increase RAM for that pool

why_it_matters: Health endpoints show whether workers are ready and connected to DB/Redis
warning_signal: Worker health fails or readiness flaps
action: Check DB, Redis, encryption key, and worker resource limits

why_it_matters: External providers care about response time and timeout
warning_signal: p95 approaches provider timeout or retry behavior appears
action: Return early, move heavy processing async, and add concurrency controls

why_it_matters: HTTP errors reflect runtime, proxy, queue, or workflow failure
warning_signal: 5xx rate rises during traffic bursts
action: Inspect ingress logs, n8n logs, worker logs, and DB/Redis health

why_it_matters: Binary storage can fill filesystem, database, or object storage
healthy_signal: Growth matches expected retention and pruning
warning_signal: Storage grows despite pruning or approaches quota
action: Confirm active binary mode, tune retention, add lifecycle, or externalize storage

why_it_matters: Filesystem mode and temp files depend on durable disk capacity
warning_signal: Above 75 percent or fast growth in binaryData/temp paths
action: Clean temp files, tune pruning, expand volume, or move to S3/database mode

why_it_matters: S3 or external storage errors break binary read/write after execution
warning_signal: 403, 404, timeout, or region errors appear
action: Check bucket policy, credentials, region, lifecycle, and mode migration

why_it_matters: AI calls can dominate execution time even when local CPU is fine
healthy_signal: p95 stable and within workflow timeout
warning_signal: Latency rises or retries increase
action: Add backoff, rate limit, fallback model, and dedicated AI workers

why_it_matters: Model 429 and 5xx errors create retry storms and long executions
warning_signal: 429 or 5xx increases during bursts
action: Throttle, retry with backoff, lower concurrency, and monitor provider limits

why_it_matters: Browser automation can be a heavyweight process class
healthy_signal: Duration stable and process exits cleanly
warning_signal: Duration rises, timeouts occur, or processes remain
action: Limit browser concurrency, isolate worker pool, and clean temp files

why_it_matters: Screenshots, downloads, and caches can fill disk
healthy_signal: Temp storage returns to baseline after jobs
warning_signal: Temp directory grows after completed executions
action: Add cleanup, set download directory policy, and monitor temp volume

why_it_matters: Backup is required for PostgreSQL and binary-heavy recovery
warning_signal: Backup failure or missing backup
action: Fix backup immediately and pause risky upgrades until backup is healthy

why_it_matters: Backup duration reveals database and binary data growth
warning_signal: Duration expands into business hours or exceeds RTO assumption
action: Tune retention, split binary storage, increase backup resources, and test restore

why_it_matters: Restore drill proves the plan works, not just backup creation
warning_signal: Restore is untested or takes longer than accepted downtime
action: Run restore drill, document gaps, and reduce retained data if needed

why_it_matters: Logs are first evidence for DB, queue, storage, credentials, and API errors
warning_signal: Same error repeats or grows by release/workflow
action: Create alert, assign owner, and connect to the relevant runbook
